subject: Human Resources
title: The Employee Handbook
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Employee Handbook.

hook.text: An employee is fired for something they didn't know was against policy. There was no handbook.

buildup.text: The employee handbook documents company policies, expectations, benefits, and procedures in one place.

discovery.text: It covers attendance, dress code, anti-harassment, leave, discipline, and termination—setting clear ground rules.

twist.text: A handbook also protects the company legally. Without one, enforcing policies becomes arbitrary and risky.

climax.text: The best handbooks are clear, updated annually, and written in plain language—not legalese.

quiz.question: Why is an employee handbook important?
options: It sets clear expectations and provides legal protection
It's only needed for large companies
It replaces the need for managers
